characters.description: A seven-year-old child of Feylon nearing his first tiering. He plans to claim an imperial tithe, serve one year in the army, and then return to help his village.
characters.name: Jiran

characters.description: Jiran's tier-three uncle, a former army soldier who runs Feylon's courier shop and can use a spear technique.
characters.name: Jiran's uncle

characters.description: Jiran's father, who supports a plan to prepare his son for army service through spear and hunter training.
characters.name: Jiran's father

characters.description: Jiran's mother, who joins his father in setting a training plan to prepare their son for army service.
characters.name: Jiran's mother

characters.description: Jiran's tier-one cousin, one season older than him and the daughter of his uncle. She protects Jiran from hostile apprentices by invoking imperial law.
characters.name: Niya

characters.description: An unidentified high-tier noble who seeks someone at Jiran's uncle's courier shop. His mind-affecting whisper incapacitates Niya, but Jiran resists it.
characters.name: Unidentified noble

characters.description: The newly installed provincial magistrate and the powerful noble previously encountered at Jiran's uncle's shop. He protects Jiran from immediate danger, expands the uncle's courier duties, and takes a particular interest in Jiran's condition.
characters.name: Feylon Samris

characters.description: A seamstress whose family gathers eggs along the cove ridges. She arrives with several workers and a cart of provisions for Samris's manor, helping establish the household where Jiran will live under the magistrate's protection.
characters.name: Sharel

characters.description: A deceased man from Earth whose memories remain accessible to Jiran after the failed tiering. Those memories give Jiran a store of advanced scientific ideas, which he begins preserving in coded form as recalling them becomes easier.
characters.name: Brandon

characters.description: An elderly noble who reveals that he has been listening after Jiran reports a new way to absorb the chaotic density of Densoon.
characters.name: Elderly noble

characters.description: An elderly noble who tests Jiran's judgment and accepts him as a mentee. He makes the search for hidden truths a condition of their relationship and refuses to permit Jiran's next tier until the boy achieves two-way communication with mana.
characters.name: Lenton

characters.description: An assassin who arranged a trap for several gifted children. Their implanted escape talismans deliver them into his custody after shadow-like attackers overwhelm their protector, and he takes them away under the claim that they are going to a new home.
characters.name: Markhiss

characters.description: One of the gifted children caught in a mausoleum trap with two companions. He uses an implanted escape talisman when shadow-like attackers overwhelm the group's sworn protector, only to discover that the device has delivered him into Markhiss's custody.
characters.name: Dommell

characters.description: A noble child held by Markhiss alongside Dommell and another captive. After being transported to a coastal forest, she is coerced into promising obedience and witnesses the power of the person controlling them.
characters.name: Oliviala LeCruex

characters.description: Oliviala LeCruex's cousin and a fellow prisoner of Markhiss. He is brought with her and Dommell to a decayed inland village, then confined in a cramped blue-metal cage that rapidly drains their mana and leaves all three children powerless.
characters.name: Cameron

characters.description: A powerful crowned man who arrives after the hidden settlement's barrier falls. He checks on Olive, prompts Markhiss to withdraw, and is addressed by Lenton as emperor.
characters.name: Emperor
